what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment on another board item) in which Tim agrees with the 'address as node' framing and suggests the principle should be documented centrally.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board:// URI address, type=note, source=claude_code, author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to board://item-bd30758f.
The body is a short markdown note tagged [point].
Tim affirms that Node = address and that more address kinds will emerge as exploration continues.
The note explicitly states there is no 'right way' yet.
It proposes that the principle of addresses-as-nodes should be central somewhere.
standouts: Structured link relation 'commented_on' pointing to board://item-bd30758f.
Assertion that addresses-as-nodes should be a central principle.
Acknowledgment that the taxonomy of address kinds is still evolving.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with board:// URI identifiers.
Uses a typed note record with state=posted and history transition.
Uses a markdown body prefixed with a [point] tag.
Records authorship via author_session and links via a structured list.
concerns: The phrase 'central somewhere' is vague and names no concrete location or owner.
The note is speculative and contains no actionable next step.
No code symbols or concrete implementation details are present.
notes: A design-discussion note where Tim seconds the address-as-node idea and flags that the principle deserves central documentation.
questions: Where exactly should the addresses-as-nodes principle be made central?
What are the 19 kinds of address mentioned?
What is the content of the parent item board://item-bd30758f?
What does 'central somewhere' mean in this system — a doc, a schema, a registry?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is labeled and structured as a note/comment, and it functions exactly as a comment on another board item.
intention_signals: The note appears to be trying to validate a conceptual model and nudge the team to elevate the addresses-as-nodes principle to a canonical, centrally discoverable place.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI-addressed, structured note with a typed link relation for informal design discussion.
contribution: This file contributes an explicit endorsement of the address-as-node concept and a call to centralize that principle, preserving a stakeholder's stance during an exploratory design phase.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on an address-as-node design point, agreeing with the framing and suggesting the principle be documented centrally.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with the address-as-node model and advocate for making that principle a central, discoverable design tenet.
